Summary: Signal with a long name or no label, when connected to an Outport block, can cause a crash
Description: A crash can occur when both  these situations occur: The Outport block has the Icon display  parameter set to either Signal name  or Port number and signal name . The signal entering the Outport block has a name longer than 66 characters or the signal has no label. (For example, you connect a Bus Selector signal to an Outport block and the signal has a long name (more than 66 characters) or no label.)
Workaround: You can prevent this issue by doing one of the following actions:Change the Icon display parameter of the Outport block to be Port number.Use a name with less than 66 characters for the signal entering the Outport block.

Summary: emlc and emlmex generate incorrect MEX files with the Watcom compiler
Description: This bug occurs when you use the mex -setup  command to select the Watcom compiler. The bug is caused by an incompatibility between the Watcom and Microsoft Visual C/C++ compilers. MEX files generated by emlc  and emlmex  use a dynamic library called libemlrt.dll , which was compiled with Microsoft Visual C/C++. This incompatibility causes certain functions in libemlrt.dll  to compute wrong answers when called from a MEX file generated with Watcom.
Workaround: Replace the file emlrt.h, located in the subfolder extern\include of the MATLAB distribution, with the new version in the attachment to this bug report. The new version includes pragmas that fix the incompatibility between Watcom and Microsoft Visual C/C++ compilers:#ifdef __WATCOMC__#pragma aux emlrtIntegerCheckR2009b value [8087];#pragma aux emlrtNotNanCheckR2009b value [8087];#pragma aux emlrtNonNegativeCheckR2009b value [8087];#endif

Summary: Failure when linking code generated for model reference variants
Description: When generating code for a model hierarchy that utilizes model reference variants with preprocessor conditionals, the link of the top-level application might fail if there is a submodel referenced from multiple variants. The link error states that functions corresponding to that submodel are missing.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real-Time Workshop might generate invalid code for Selector block operating on n-dimensional signal
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate invalid code for a model under the following conditions: A Selector block indexes into a n-dimensional signal The source of the n-dimensional signal is a virtual Bus Selector block The virtual bus signal source is an input port of an Enable and Triggered Subsystem block One or more bus signal elements connect directly to an output port of the Enabled and Triggered Subsystem block  The Inport block becomes a nonvirtual operation on the bus signal, if one of its elements connects directly to an Outport block. In the generated code, the bus signal is copied into an array for this nonvirtual operation. The offset into this array is miscalculated if a Selector block is operating on n-dimensional bus signal elements. Avoid connecting a signal directly from an Inport block to an Outport block in triggered subsystems. The nonvirtual copy operation might be expensive when only a subset of the bus signal connects to the Outport block. To avoid this issue, insert a Signal Conversion block in front of the Outport block.
Workaround: The attached MATLAB script finds any occurrence of a Selector block that generates invalid code and returns an array of block handles for those Selector blocks. The input argument is the model name. After running the script, use the hilite_system command to highlight the Selector block in the model diagram. The input argument to hilite_system is a block handle. See the following example:>> blkHdls = findIvalidSelectorBlocks('<model_name>')blkHdls =16.0010 17.0010>> hilite_system(blkHdls(1))If a model is not affected by this issue, this script returns an empty array. A workaround to this problem is to insert a Signal Conversion block at the first input port of the offending Selector block.

Summary: Report Generator may lose unsaved Microsoft Word documents
Description: When using Report Generator to generate a Word document, open instances of Microsoft Word (including those launched outside of the MATLAB environment) may close silently and lose any unsaved work.
Workaround: To correct this issue, apply the attached patch:1. Close MATLAB.2. From the MATLAB installation directory, navigate to toolbox\rptgen\rptgen.3. Rename docview.m to docview.m.bak.4. Copy the attached docview.m into the directory indicated above.5. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Folder removal errors might occur during diagram update or build that triggers automatic parallel model reference build
Description: In a parallel computing environment, initiating a diagram update or build for your model might produce temporary-folder removal errors if both of the following conditions exist: Your model contains a model reference hierarchy. Conditions allow referenced models to be built in parallel.  The parallel build displays error messages similar to the following: C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087\model\slprj\sim could not be removed.C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087\model\slprj could not be removed.C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087\model could not be removed.C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087 could not be removed.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB might produce wrong answers when embedded hardware setting specifies integer size different from that of MATLAB host or test hardware.
Description: Embedded MATLAB might produce wrong answers during simulation and RTW code generation when calling library functions whose implementations rely on the C function frexp  in the following situations: In simulation, the integer size specified for the embedded hardware is different from the integer size of the MATLAB host. In RTW code generation without emulation hardware, the integer size specified for embedded hardware is different from the integer size of the MATLAB host. In RTW code generation with emulation hardware, the embedded hardware and emulation hardware settings specify different integer sizes.  The following library functions might be affected: betainc , colon , eps , expint , expm , filter , gemmainc , hist , ismember , intersect , issorted , log2 , log10 , median , nchoosek , quadgk , quad2d , rank , sort , sortrows , unique , randperm , setdiff , setxor , and union .

Summary: Unexpected termination or wrong sample time reported by rate transition diagnostic
Description: The rate transition diagnostic reports an incorrect sample time if the destination block of the rate transition is a subsystem. If, in addition, the following conditions apply, then Simulink terminates unexpectedly. The source block has multiple outputs. The output port number for the source block is larger than the number of destination output ports.
Workaround: To avoid an unexpected termination of Simulink, disable the diagnostic on the Solvers pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box by either:Turning on "Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer".Changing the "Tasking mode for periodic sample times" to "SingleTasking" and setting the "Single task rate transition" to "none".?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: data consistency issue for malloc-based targets with multiple model instances
Description: Generated code for malloc-based targets potentially can corrupt global pointer variables if you create two or more instances of a model in the same executable. Code is generated that might corrupt data if all of the following conditions are true: You generate code for a malloc-based target, either grt_malloc.tlc , or tornado.tlc  with Tornado Target  pane parameter Code Format  set to RealTimeMalloc .  You customized your code to create two or more instances of a model in the same executable.  You customized your code to preemptively schedule those model instances on a single processor or concurrently schedule the instances across multiple cores.  A parent subsystem in the model explicitly sets the subsystem parameter Real-Time Workshop system code  to Reusable function .  That parent subsystem has at least one child subsystem that cannot be parented by a reusable subsystem for one of the following reasons: The child subsystem is a function-call subsystem and is called by a wide (nonscalar) function call signal. The child subsystem is a function-call subsystem and is called by a noninlined S-function. The child subsystem contains a noninlined S-function.    In this modeling scenario, the build process generates global pointer variables that potentially can be corrupted when an instance of a model is preemptively or concurrently scheduled with other instances of the same model. Note:  The following modeling approaches are not affected   by this issue: Preemptively schedule rates within the same model instance (multitasking solver mode with rate monotonic scheduling). Use Model blocks to combine multiple instances of the same model into one model hierarchy and one generated executable. Use the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder target option Generate reusable code  and customize the target to create multiple instances of the same model.
Workaround: To work around this bug, open the parent subsystem in which the parameter Real-Time Workshop system code is explicitly set to Reusable function and change the setting to Auto.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions may incorrectly create shared utilities, resulting in uncompilable code.
Description: If you enable shared utilities and use parameters in Embedded MATLAB code in Simulink or Stateflow, your model might generate uncompilable code. When this bug occurs, the C compiler reports an error about undeclared identifiers.

Summary: Cannot export Function-Call Subsystem or Triggered Subsystem from a model that uses simplified initialization mode
Description: If the parameter 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection' is set to 'Simplified', the following error message appears when you export a Function-Call Subsystem from the model: "The parameter 'Source of initial output value:' of Outport block 'rtwdemo_subsystem/__ExpFcn__1/Out1' must be set to 'Input signal' because a function-call trigger signal from output port 1 of block 'rtwdemo_subsystem/__ExpFcn__1/expFcn' is passing through the Outport block." Additionally, in R2009b, the following error message appears when you export a Triggered Subsystem from the model: "The parameter 'Source of initial output value:' of Outport block 'Subsystem_expfcn/Subsystem/__ConvTrig2FcnCallFcnCall__2/sys/Out1' must be set to 'Input signal' because a function-call trigger signal from output port 1 of block 'Subsystem_expfcn/Subsystem/__ConvTrig2FcnCallFcnCall__2/sys/expFcn' is passing through the Outport block."
Workaround: Locate the folder where the MATLAB software is installed using the command matlabroot.Replace the file matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/slbus.p with the attached patch file associated with your version of MATLAB.For R2009b, also replace the file matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/slPrepMdlForExportFcn.p with the attached patch file.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Model reference with bus at root outport might cause incorrect simulation and code generation result
Description: Referenced models might produce incorrect simulation and code generation results under the following conditions: The Outport block of a conditional subsystem or function call subsystem connects to a input of a Bus Creator block The Bus Creator block connects to the model's root Outport block, which has a bus object  ? See the attached example model, mRootOutLocalBug.mdl , which references a model that meets the above conditions. The Real-Time Workshop generated code does not produce the same result as simulation.
Workaround: In the parent model, mark the model block's output port as a testpoint.You can fix the the attached example model, mRootOutLocalBug.mdl, using this workaround. See mFix.mdl, for details.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For models that pass reusable subsystem outputs as individual arguments
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate invalid code for a model containing reusable subsystems under all of the following conditions: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual arguments . The reusable Subsystem blocks connect to a Rate Transition block.  ?
Workaround: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the parameter Pass reusable subsystem outputs as to Structure reference, which is the default value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Answer: Under certain circumstances, code generated from Stateflow charts containing Simulink functions might be wrong
Description: Under the following conditions, incorrect simulation and Real-Time Workshop code generation for a Stateflow chart might occur: The chart resides in a library model and is being used inside another model for which code is being generated. The chart contains a Simulink function-call subsystem. The chart has input events.
Workaround: NOTE: The following workaround requires access to the MATLAB installation area.Find out the MATLAB version you are using. You can find this out using the following command on the MATLAB prompt:versionDepending on the version of MATLAB you are using, download one of the attached files: autobuild_driver_8b.m, autobuild_driver_9a.m, autobuild_driver_9b.mRename this file to autobuild_driver.mOn the MATLAB prompt, type:which private/autobuild_driver.mReplace this file with the attached file.Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Real-Time Workshop generated C code for Unary Minus or Switch block might not compile
Description: Real-Time Workshop C code generation of the Unary Minus or Switch block might produce uncompilable code. The generated code might have an empty condition and an uncompilable assignment statement, similar to the following code pattern: if (rtb_Input) {} else {= rtb_Input;}  If the Unary Minus or Switch block connects to an S-Function block, the probability of this error increases.
Workaround: Do either of the following:Turn off expression folding for the model by clearing the Eliminate superfluous temporary variables (Expression folding) parameter on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Place a Signal Conversion block at the outport of the Unary Minus or Switch block and select the Signal Conversion block parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Error when interacting with Simulink models and missing paths after invoking 'restoredefaultpath'.
Description: Invoking the command 'restoredefaultpath' on a system that has Embedded IDE Link installed results in the following behavior: If you open a Simulink model and click on the word 'Tools' in the menu bar, the MATLAB software throws the error:    ??? Undefined function or method 'util_isMWSoftwareInstalled' for   input arguments of type 'char'.  Some Embedded IDE Link functionalities do not work properly because of missing paths. The missing paths are:    [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator   [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator\hookpoints   [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\foundation\autointerface\util   [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\ccslinkblks
Workaround: Download and unzip the attached file.Copy the file, g596680_9b_patch.exe, to your MATLAB root.Double-click the file.In the dialog that opens, click Unzip. You should get the message "2 file(s) unzipped successully."

Summary: Toggling block reduction setting changes numeric behavior of model with Saturation block
Description: The Saturation block gives the wrong numerical results for simulation and code generation when the following conditions are true: Block reduction is on. The upper limit of the Saturation block is greater than the maximum value that the input data type can represent. The lower limit of the Saturation block is less than the minimum value that the input data type can represent. The input and output of the Saturation block have different data types.

Summary: Communication between the xPC Target host PC and target PC is slow (on the order of 3 to 4 Mbps)
Description: Communication between the xPC Target host PC and target PC is slow (on the order of 3 to 4 Mbps). You will likely experience slow communication only when no real-time model is running on the target PC.
Workaround: As a workaround:Ignore the issue. You will likely experience slow communication only when no real-time model is running on the target PC.Contact MathWorks Technical Support for software patches for xPC Target Version 4.1 (R2009a) or 4.2 (R2009b).

Summary: sort with complex array input may return an incorrect result on Windows platforms.
Description: When X  is a complex array (not a vector), sort(X)  may return an incorrect result when size(X,dim) > 10 , where dim  is the dimension being sorted along. This bug affects only Windows platforms (both 32- and 64-bit).
Workaround: Since sort works properly with complex vectors, a workaround for this bug is to call sort in a for loop. For example, if X is a complex matrix, Y = sort(X) can be done correctly with the following code:Y = X;for k = 1:size(X,2)    Y(:,k) = sort(X(:,k));endA more general solution for complex N-D arrays sorting over arbitrary dimensions (i.e. Y = sort(X, dim)) looks like:dimvec = [dim, 1:dim-1, dim+1:ndims(X)];Y = permute(X, dimvec);sortLen = size(Y, 1);for k = 1:sortLen:numel(Y)    Y(k:k+sortLen-1) = sort(Y(k:k+sortLen-1), 1);endY = ipermute(Y, dimvec);

Summary: AUTOSAR model with multirunnables does not build when there is an instance of a reusable subsystem in different runnables
Description: An AUTOSAR multirunnable model with instances of a reusable subsystem in different runnables will generate the following build error: -----------------------------------------------------### Initial pass through model to cache user defined code.### Caching model source code......Error: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\syslib.tlc Line: 1353 Column: 34The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifierError: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 183 Column: 17The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same typeError: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 185 Column: 21The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same typeError: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 188 Column: 21The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same type### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model' aborted due to an error.??? Subsystem build failed
Workaround: Do one of the following:Inline the reusable subsystem.Use the attached .tlc file to replace your current .tlc file.

Summary: sort(X,dim)may give an incorrect result when dim > 1 and dim < ndims(X).
Description: For an N-D array X , sort(X,dim)  does not sort the array correctly when dim > 1  and dim < ndims(X) . sort  works properly when size(X,dim)  is sufficiently small (less than 100) regardless of the value of dim . Note that because of the restriction on dim , this bug does not affect sort  on 2-D arrays.
Workaround: A workaround is to permute the array prior to sorting and permute the result back. Consider replacing Y = sort(X, dim)with the following code:dimvec = 1:ndims(X);dimvec(dim) = [];Y = sort(permute(X,[dim dimvec]),1);Y = ipermute(Y, [dim dimvec]);?

Summary: When a model has a Signal Builder block with a signal group that has no links to requirements, an error occurs when creating the requirements report.
Description: User gets the following error when trying to generate a requirements report: ??? Error using ==> locGenerateReport at 146Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. Error in ==> D:\archive\R2009b\ship\win32\dist\toolbox\matlab\iofun\@rptgen\report.p>report at 51 Error in ==> report at 47varargout = rptgen.report(varargin{:}); ?
Workaround: You can patch this problem by updating one of the files in the MATLAB installation:1) cd <matlabroot>\R2009b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\@RptgenRMI\@CReqSigbGroupLoop where <matlabroot> is the installation directory.2) Back up the file loop_getLoopObjects.p to a new directory that is not on the MATLAB path.3) Copy the attached file into the directory.4) Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Errors occur when building xPC Target models that contain Embedded MATLAB blocks configured with variable inputs and varaible outputs
Description: Errors occur when building xPC Target models that contain Embedded MATLAB blocks configured with variable inputs and varaible outputs. The model fails to build and the following error message is displayed: Error: File: \matlabroot \toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\mdlxml.tlc: 243 Column: 52Ambiguous reference to ChartData - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopes
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached file tlc9b.zip and unzip it to a temporary location.Change directory to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc.From this folder, delete mdlxml.tlc and capi_xpc.tlc.Copy and paste the unzipped files mdlxml.tlc and capi_xpc.tlc from the temporary location to the tlc folder.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: SegV produced when simulating a model containing Stateflow Truth Tables in parfor loop
Description: When a Stateflow Truth Table model is simulated inside a parfor loop, it may produce a SegV and crash MATLAB. This bug usually happens on Windows 64-bit platforms.
Workaround: Before simulating the model using parfor, make sure the model has been resaved using the following steps:In the main MATLAB session, open the Truth Table model.Update diagram.Resave the model, including all used library models that contain Truth Tables.

Summary: Incorrect CONSTANT-REF for arrays in exported AUTOSAR datatype arxml file
Description: For Simulink models that have wide inports and outports, the exported datatype arxml file contains incorrect CONSTANT-REF  values. For example, <CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_SInt32_2InitValue</SHORT-NAME> <VALUE> <ARRAY-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_SInt32_2InitValue</SHORT-NAME> <TYPE-TREF DEST="ARRAY-TYPE">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/rt_Array_SInt32_2</TYPE-TREF> <ELEMENTS> <CONSTANT-REFERENCE> <SHORT-NAME>SInt32_1</SHORT-NAME> <TYPE-TREF DEST="INTEGER-TYPE">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/SInt32</TYPE-TREF> <CONSTANT-REF DEST="CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/Ground/SInt32InitValue/SInt32InitValue< /CONSTANT-REF> </CONSTANT-REFERENCE>   In this example, CONSTANT-REF should be: <CONSTANT-REF DEST="CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/Ground/SInt32InitValue</CONSTANT-REF>
Workaround: Either edit the datatype arxml file and specify the correct value for CONSTANT-REF, or:Download the attached file.Use this file to replace the following file: matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@exporter/private/p_export_component.pBuild your model to regenerate the datatype arxml file.

Summary: Incorrect coverage for MinMax blocks with fixed-point signals or different data types
Description: When your model contains MinMax blocks with fixed-point signals or MinMax blocks that have different data types for the inputs and outputs, the reported coverage might be incorrect.

Summary: Data Type Conversion block configured as "stored integer" emits data copies when connected to an S-function block
Description: Suppose that a fixed-point signal, such as fixdt(1,16,15), is passed through a Data Type Conversion block configured to output an int16 value as a stored integer. In R2009a, this Data Type Conversion block is optimized away in the generated code.  However, in R2009b, the Data Type Conversion block introduces data copies if connected to an S-function block that uses TLC for code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: generated C++ encapsulation interface for referenced model might produce incorrect numerical result
Description: Generated code results for your model might not match simulation results for your model if both of the following conditions exist: Your model contains at least one Model block. You configured a C++ encapsulation interface for the model referenced by the Model block.  Under these conditions, in the generated code for the model, the parent model output function might call the referenced model's output function with incorrect input arguments. As a result, execution of the code might generate an incorrect numerical result.

Summary: The RS-232 hardware receives signals of random FIFO overflow errors and becomes unresponsive
Description: The RS-232 hardwares receive signals of random FIFO overflow errors and becomes unresponsive. All composite xPC Target RS-232 subsystem blocks stop and unpredictably generate FIFO overflows. This behavior generates an error and halts the model. This behavior affects all blocks for the supported Quatech, mainboard, Diamond Systems, and Commtech Fastcom boards.
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached file, fifowrite.c, to the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocksRestart MATLAB.Change folder to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.In the MATLAB Command Window, type mex fifowrite.cChange back to your project directory and rebuild your model.

Summary: PID Controller and PID Controller (2DOF) blocks may not initialize correctly when model loads
Description: The PID Controller  and PID Controller (2DOF)  blocks may not initialize correctly when the Simulink? model loads. Under some conditions, the blocks load with the library block default under-mask circuits instead of the circuit specified by the parameters in the block dialog. In particular, if the block is not visible when the model loads (for example, the block is inside a Subsystem  block), the block will not initialize correctly.
Workaround: Unzip the attached file, and apply the attached patch as follows:Exit MATLAB?.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID.m.old. $matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine. To locate $matlabroot, type matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID2DOF.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID2DOF.m.old.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\PIDConfig.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\PIDConfig.m.old.Save the attached files to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type the following command to complete the installation:>> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Simulink Designer Verifier fails to generate a report when Report Generator is not installed.
Description: Simulink Designer Verifiers fails to generate a report. >>sldvreport('fileName') returns unsuccessfully Status = 0 Second argument = ''
Workaround: 1) Locate the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen, where <matlabroot> is the installation directory for MATLAB.2) Move the the file report.p to a backup location that is not on the MATLAB path.3) Copy the attached file report.p into the directory.4) Locate the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@cfr_paragraph/, where <matlabroot> is the installation directory for MATLAB.5) Move the the file execute.p to a backup location that is not on the MATLAB path.6) Copy the attached file execute.p into the directory.7) Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Functions returning arrays of complex numbers in Embedded MATLAB or Stateflow may generate incorrect code
Description: An Embedded MATLAB function that returns an array of complex numbers may generate incorrect code when the calling function uses the array once in a single indexing expression. For example, the following code might cause this bug : ...A = foo();% A is a complex matrix.t = A(i); The bug may also occur if the variable has multiple uses, but optimizations usually eliminate all but one of the uses. For example, adding a third line, temp = A(i) , where temp  is never used, will not solve the problem because this line will be eliminated as useless code.
Workaround: Immediately after calling the function, add a conditional that:Is not constantAlways evaluate to falseContains code that assigns a value to the array of complex numbersFor instance, rewrite the above example as follows:...A = foo();if x == y % This condition must be false and not constant  A(:) = 0;endt = A(i);For this workaround to be effective, the if statement must appear in the generated code.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier crashes if the Lookup method of a Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) is set to Interpolation-Extrapolation and the Input/Output interface of the block has different floating-point types.
Description: If the Lookup method of Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) block is set to Interpolation-Extrapolation and the Input/Output interface of the block has different floating-point types (for example, the input signal has type single and the output signal has type double), Simulink Design Verifer crashes when checking compatibility.
Workaround: 1. Back up these files to a location outside the MATLAB path:matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32.csf2. Download the attached files:slavteng.mexw32slavteng.mexw32.csf3. Save the downloaded files in matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv, replacing the existing versions of those files.

Summary: Model with duplicate "Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation" blocks does not build
Description: When a model contains multiple "Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation" blocks with the same Client port name, Operation prototype, and Interface path, the model does not build because a validation check fails with the following error: Last validation failed.Error: the Data Element/Operation "op" of the Interface "/AUTOSAR/Interface" cannot be duplicated for AUTOSAR Port "port"
Workaround: Do the following:Download the attached files.Use these files to replace the following files:matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@exporter/private/p_export_component.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@AutosarInterface/runValidation.pBuild your model.?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier crashes if constant values are passed as inputs to Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) blocks where the input or output ports are type single or double.
Description: If the input to a Lookup Table block or any of the inputs to a Lookup Table (2-D) block is a constant, Simulink Design Verifier crashes during checking compatibility of the model. This failure happens only if any of the input or output types of the Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) block are type single or double.
Workaround: 1. Backup these two files to a location outside the MATLAB path:matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32.csf2. Download the attached files:slavteng.mexw32slavteng.mexw32.csf3. Save the downloaded files to matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv, replacing the existing files.

Summary: Code generated from AUTOSAR Model with non-scalar Constant block connected to Invoke Server Operation block may not compile
Description: If an AUTOSAR model contains a Constant block that sends a non-scalar signal to an Invoke Server Operation block, then the generated code may fail to compile. The compilation error refers to an attempt to take the address of an address, for example, Rte_Call_port_op1(&&Model_ConstP.C1_Value[0]);
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Apply a testpoint to the output signal of the Constant block.Place a Signal Conversion block on the output signal of the Constant block. Select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization for the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Uncompilable code from AUTOSAR server operation model with internal states that reference buses where header file is not set to 'Rte_Type.h'
Description: The code generated for your AUTOSAR Server operation model will not compile if the following are all true: Your model has internal states, for example, it contains a unit-delay block with a state attribute. A state attribute references a bus object whose header file property is not set to 'Rte_Type.h' .  You see the following compilation errors: c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(23) : error C2016: C requires that a struct or union has at least one memberc:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(23) : error C2061: syntax error : identifier 'mystruct'c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(27) : error C2059: syntax error : '}'c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_mbusmodel.h(34) : error C2143: syntax error : missing '{' before '*'mbusmodel.c(22) : error C2143: syntax error : missing ')' before '*'mbusmodel.c(22) : error C2143: syntax error : missing '{' before '*'mbusmodel.c(22) : error C2371: 'real_T' : redefinition; different basic types c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\rtwtypes.h(63) : see declaration of 'real_T'
Workaround: Set the header file of the bus object to 'Rte_Type', for example,mystruct.HeaderFile = 'Rte_Type.h'

Summary: Configuring multiple AUTOSAR runnables may fail
Description: Configuring multiple AUTOSAR runnables may fail if all of the following apply: You have not previously configured the runnables. The wrapper subsystem contains a Merge block that combines the outputs of the runnables. You select the button "Get Default Configuration".  As a result of the failure, you see an error message similar to the following: 'Model /Subystem ' is being invoked with multiple asynchronous rates that cannot be combined. To combine multiple asynchronous rates, assign the same priority to the rates and ensure that all rates use the base rate timer.
Workaround: One workaround is as follows:Before you configure the runnables for the first time, delete the Merge block.If the model will no longer compile, continue to make minimal, temporary modifications until you are able to compile it.Configure the multiple runnables, and click Apply or OK.Restore the Merge block and undo any other temporary model modifications that you made.Once you have carried out an initial configuration of the runnables, the issue no longer arises.

Summary: When solving linear systems using mldivide (\) and ctranspose (') together (as A'\b), MATLAB? may return incorrect results if A is a permutation of a triangular matrix.
Description: When solving a linear system that involves mldivide  and the conjugate transpose of a matrix A  (as A'\b ), MATLAB? returns an incorrect result if the matrix A  meets all of the following conditions: A  is square and full, A  is not a scalar, A  is a permutation of a triangular matrix, A  is not a lower-triangular matrix, A  is not an upper-triangular matrix.
Workaround: To work around the issue, perform the conjugate transpose of the matrix separately from the division. Change:x = A'\b;toAt = A';x = At\b;

Summary: Matrix multiplication (mtimes) may return incorrect results on certain 32-bit processors when MATLAB? is set to single-threaded mode.
Description: When MATLAB? is set to use a single computational thread, mtimes  may return an incorrect result on certain 32-bit processors. To determine if your processor may be affected by this: Start MATLAB using the -singleCompThread  startup flag. Execute the following commands:a = rand(2000);b = rand(2000);oneThread = a*b;save wrkspace;  Exit MATLAB and restart MATLAB normally (without using the -singleCompThread  startup flag). Execute the following commands in the new session:load wrkspace;autoThread = a*b;tol = 100*eps(class(autoThread))*size(a,1) %use a reasonable tolerancenumericallyCorrect = max(abs(oneThread(:)-autoThread(:))) < tol   If the value of numericallyCorrect  is not 1 (true ), this bug may be present for your processor.
Workaround: Contact technical support to obtain a patch that can resolve this bug.https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html

Summary: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might fail to generate code for S-Function block under specific configuration
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might fail to generate code for a model containing a S-function block and you select the following configuration parameters: Simulink block descriptions  on the Real-Workshop > Comments  pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box Eliminate superflous local variables (Expression folding)  on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box  The following error message displays: Error: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\mw\commentlib.tlc Line: 405 Column: 121Undefined identifier origblockError: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\mw\commentlib.tlc Line: 54 Column: 65Undefined identifier blockError: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\mw\commentlib.tlc Line: 187 Column: 27Undefined identifier blockError: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 2339 Column: 15 Undefined identifier block
Workaround: Clear one of the following configuration parameters:Simulink block descriptions on the Real-Workshop > Comments pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog boxEliminate superflous local variables (Expression folding) on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box

Summary: Highlighting of Stateflow simulation initial states can fail
Description: The active state in the Stateflow simulation is not highlighted when both these conditions apply: Chart property "Execute (enter) Chart At Initialization" is enabled. No transition is taken after the initial states are entered.
Workaround: Back up matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/remove_hilite.mDownload the attached zip file and extract it to matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/remove_hilite.m

Summary: Segmentation violation when mapping shift-left operator output to replacement function input argument using custom target function library
Description: During code generation for a model that contains a shift-left operation, the software might generate a segmentation violation if all of the following conditions exist: You registered a shift-left replacement function in a custom target function library (TFL) and selected the TFL for the model. In the TFL table entry, you specified that the shift-left replacement function takes the output as a function argument, for example, "myShiftLeft(u1, &y1); ". The shift-left operation in the model meets the TFL match criteria and triggers the replacement.

Summary: UDP blocks do not work when target PCs are configured to use multicore processors
Description: UDP blocks do not work when target PCs are configured to use multicore processors. In these situations, these blocks cannot send or receive data.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:Disable multicore processor support and use single core processor support.Contact MathWorks technical support for an R2009b (xPC Target Version 4.2) softwware patch.

Summary: Simulink may crash for models containing function-call Model blocks with bus I/O
Description: If a model contains a function-call Model block, then the model may crash during simulation or code generation if either of the following is true: The referenced model has a bus input, and there is a virtual bus entering the corresponding inport on the Model block. The referenced model has a bus output, and the output port has the parameter Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model  set to off .  The crash may have a stack trace that contains these frames: Stack Trace:  [0] libmwsimulink.dll:_gg_window(0x5645ee20 "BlockDiagram_tag@@@Z", 0, 0x57469c20, 0x5645ee20 "BlockDiagram_tag@@@Z") + 6 bytes   [1] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl MSW_DeleteBlockToolTip(class slBlock const *) (0x57469c20, 0x57469dc0, 0x57469c20, 0x56d3b020 "?v?V0?@W") + 115 bytes   [2] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl sgb_owner(class slBlock *,struct slGraph_tag *) (0x57469c20, 0, 0x57469c20, 0x56d3b020 "?v?V0?@W") + 52 bytes   [3] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl RemoveBlocksInsertedBfProp(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *)(0x25447880, 0x49188680, 0x25447880, 0x25447880) + 137 bytes   [4] libmwsimulink.dll:public: void __thiscall slModel::shutdownModel(void)(0x49188680, 0x00d3a558, 0, 0x00d3a530 "???") + 1391 bytes  [5] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl slDestroyModel(class slModel *) (0x49188680, 0x0a362c20, 3, 0) + 94 bytes   [6] libmwsimulink.dll:__catch$?DoRTWGen@@YAPAVslsvErrMsgQueue@@PAURTWInfo@@PAUmxArray_tag@@HQAPAU3@@Z$13(0x00d3a500 "on", 0x245ec560, 3, 0x00d3a7a0) + 303 bytes

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded MATLAB functions accessing Stateflow local vector data may generate incorrect code.
Description: Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts that access Stateflow local vectors of size [n 1]  or [1 n]  may generate incorrect code in Accelerator mode or Real-Time Workshop. The following conditions may trigger the bug: At least one Embedded MATLAB function accesses the local vector using single-dimension indexing (for example, V(i) ). At least one function uses the local vector without defining it.  Due to interactions with other optimizations (primarily function inlining), some models that meet all of these conditions do not exhibit the bug. When the bug does occur, an incorrect optimization converts chart-level vectors into C stack variables (for example, auto variables). Once this incorrect conversion happens, subsequent optimizations may lead to further mangling of the code. Symptoms of this bug can range from uncompilable code, warnings in the generated code, or silent wrong answers.
Workaround: Do not use single-dimensional indexing to access chart local vectors from Embedded MATLAB functions.For example, for row-vector R of size [1 n], either use two-dimensional indexing of the form R(1,i) or change the size of R to[n]. Note, however, that the latter option converts R from a row-vector into a column-vector and may change the behavior of your MATLAB functions.For column-vector C of size [n 1], change the size to [n]. This change does not affect the behavior of your MATLAB code.The attached MATLAB files implement a Model Advisor rule that warns you of syntactic occurrences of chart local vectors in your model that may require attention. Note that this rule does not detect situations where chart local sizes are parameterized. It works only when you enter sizes literally in the size field.To add this rule to the Model Advisor, unzip the attached file, and place the resulting MATLAB files on your MATLAB path. Then, run the Model Advisor by selecting it from the Tools menu in Simulink.

Summary: Repeatedly copying and pasting Simulink(R) function from Stateflow(R) chart to Simulink model leads to multiple copies of Simulink function-call subsystem
Description: Starting from R2009b, it is possible to copy a Simulink function-call object from a Stateflow chart and paste it in a Simulink model. When this action is done, the previous contents in the Simulink clipboard are not cleared. Hence, if there were existing items in the Simulink clipboard, a subsequent paste in a model would paste the originally copied objects in addition to the newly copied Simulink function. The correct behavior is that when you copy a Simulink function from the Stateflow chart and paste it into a Simulink model, only the Simulink function is pasted in the Simulink Editor.
Workaround: The workaround below needs write access to the MATLAB(R) installation area:Download the attached SimulinkMan.mAt the MATLAB prompt, type: which Stateflow.SLINSF.SimulinkManOverwrite the file referenced by the previous command with the downloaded versionRestart MATLAB

Summary: Basic Service client ports with zero arguments cause import process to error out with non-descriptive message
Description: If a Software Component has a Basic Service port that is configured as a client with no arguments, then the import process fails with the following error message: Error using ==> p_create_component at 131Should not be here
Workaround: Either remove all operations with no arguments from the the arxml file you want to import, or do the following:Download the attached file.Use this file to replace the following file:matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@importer/private/p_create_component.p?

Summary: Using AUTOSAR target can cause data type propagation error
Description: If your model is configured for the AUTOSAR target, code generation can fail with a data type propagation error during model compilation, even though this error does not occur with a different Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder target.
Workaround: This error occurs because the software uses heuristics during data type propagation. To identify what activates these heuristics:Select Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity.Under Signals, in the Underspecified data types field, select either warning or error from the drop-down list.If you modify your model so that the software does not use heuristics, then inconsistent data type propagation will cease to be an issue in most cases.

Summary: TLC errors might occur when generating C++ encapsulation interface for referenced model invoked by Stateflow chart using function-call signal?
Description: Generating code for your model might produce TLC errors if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Stateflow chart that invokes a referenced model using a function-call signal. The referenced model is configured with a C++ encapsulation interface.  Under these conditions, code generation might produce TLC errors similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 809 Column: 59 Indices may not be negative Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 810 Column: 56 Indices may not be negative Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 811 Column: 34 Unable to find DWork within the Block scope Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 811 Column: 44 Unable to find CPPObjDWorkIdxInSubsys within the Block scope
Workaround: To work around this bug, avoid using the direct function-call signal from the Stateflow chart to the referenced model. Instead, place the referenced model inside a noninlined function-call subsystem, and make the subsystem reusable by opening the Subsystem Parameters dialog box and setting the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Reusable function.?

Summary: Failure during code generation for AUTOSAR model with sub-bus connected to root outport
Description: Code generation can fail for an AUTOSAR model that has a sub-bus connected to a root outport, for example, a Bus Selector block connected to the root outport. The code generation can fail with an assertion like the following: CGIR assertion in .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_autosar_transform.cpp(164): 'DerefExpr::isa(assignExpr->rhs())'[0] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const * const,int,char const * const)() + 46 bytes[1] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)() + 27 bytes[2] rtwcg.dll:public: void __thiscall RTWCG::RTWCGTransformForAutosar::apply(void)() + 636 bytes[3] rtwcg.dll:void __cdecl RTWCG::RTWTransformForAutosar(class RTWCG::RTWFcnConst *,class RTWCG::RTWContext *,struct PtrSet *)() + 124 bytes[4] rtwcg.dll:public: bool __thiscallRTWCG::RTWFcnConst::OptimizeAndEmitCode(int,class RTWCG::TLCOutPortInfo * const,class RTWCG::RTWCGVector &)() + 1194 bytes
Workaround: If the sub-bus is a single signal, place a Signal Conversion block between the Bus Selector block and the root outport. Right-click this Signal Conversion block and select SignalConversion Parameters from the context menu. In the Block Parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization check box.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software might generate uncompilable code for bitfield structure
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate uncompilable code under all of the following conditions: The model contains Boolean data or Stateflow charts; The model is configured to generate bitfields through one of the following parameters on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box: Pack Boolean data into bitfields , Use bitsets for storing state configuration , Use bitsets for storing Boolean data  On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the value for Tasking mode for period sample times  is Auto  or MultiTasking  Tasking mode for period sample times  is set to Auto  and the model has more than one periodic sample rates  The generated code might use the same name for two bitfield structures, which are both within the same structure. This causes a code compilation error.
Workaround: On the Optimizations pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, disable the bitfield optimizations: Pack Boolean data into bitfields, Use bitsets for storing state configuration, and Use bitsets for storing Boolean data.

Summary: Incorrect fixed-point word size determined when importing from an arxml file
Description: AUTOSAR uses the lower and upper values of integer types to deduce the word length for the datatype. However, for the AUTOSAR target, the software incorrectly imports the lower and upper values using real-world minimum and maxium values rather than the minimum and maximum values of the stored integer.
Workaround: Do the following:Download the attached files.Use these files to replace the following:matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@importer/private/p_add_data_type.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/messages/autosar/en/xlate

Summary: SIL compilation failure when AUTOSAR model has bus object that does not cross I/O boundary
Description: If you try to run a SIL simulation for an AUTOSAR model with a bus object that does not cross an I/O boundary, you see a compilation failure: ? If your AUTOSAR model has a bus object that does not cross an I/O boundary, you see a compilation failure when you: Run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation Build the model with SIL block creation enabled  For example, ### Compiling mbusmodel.ccl -c -DCRTAPI1=_cdecl -DCRTAPI2=_cdecl -nologo -GS -D_X86_=1 -DWIN32 -D_WIN32 -W3 -D_WINNT -D_WIN32_WINNT=0x0500 -DNTDDI_VERSION=0x05000000 -D_WIN32_IE=0x0500 -DWINVER=0x0500 -D_MT -D_DLL -MD /wd4996 /fp:precise /Od /Oy- -DMODEL=mbusmodel -DNUMST=1 -DNCSTATES=0 -DMAT_FILE=0 -DINTEGER_CODE=0 -DONESTEPFCN=1 -DTERMFCN=0 -DHAVESTDIO -DMULTI_INSTANCE_CODE=0 -DMT=0 mbusmodel.cmbusmodel.cc:\work\aslrtw\matlab\test\toolbox\rtw\targets\ert\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(28) : error C2371: 'mystruct' : redefinition; different basic types ..\mystruct_typedef.h(7) : see declaration of 'mystruct'NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"c:\program files\microsoft visual studio 9.0\VC\BIN\cl.EXE"' : return code '0x2'Stop.
Workaround: Turn off SIL verification for the model.

Summary: AUTOSAR validation fails to identity issue that is reported during code generation
Description: When a model uses the AUTOSAR target, the signals entering the root output ports must not have a non-Auto  storage class. However, some violations of this restriction are not identified during AUTOSAR validation. Instead, these violations are reported during code generation.

Summary: Seg fault during compatibility/translation of models with Stateflow or Embedded MATLAB
Description: When you run Simulink Design Verifier software on a model containing Stateflow transitions or Embedded MATLAB code that uses the short-circuited operators && or ||, you might encounter a seg fault with a stack trace similar to: Stack Trace:  [0] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0x100000001, 0x200000002, 0x100000004, 5) + 23 bytes  [1] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0, 0x407e79b0 "p|~@", 0x0bd0df20,  0x2b2128e6c0e0) + 63 bytes   [2] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0x407e7a50, 0x2aaacf8b7900, 0x0d178ce0, 0x407e7ca8) + 63 bytes  [3] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0x0cf38ae0 "?1j??*", 0x0cdf5def, 0x3000000010, 81) + 63 bytes  [4] libmwcgir_cgel.so:cgl_yyparse()(0x407e7ff0, 0x2aaad846d8a3, 0x0bd0df20, 0x407e83d0) + 4941 bytes    ?
Workaround: You can patch this problem on 32-bit Windows installations by replacing the Simulink Design Verfier MEX-file with the attachment in this geck.1. Backup these two files to a location outside the MATLAB path:matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32.csf2. Download the attached files:slavteng.mexw32slavteng.mexw32.csf3. Save the downloaded files to matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv, replacing the existing files.

Summary: SIM and RTW target rebuild for a referenced model may fail if the model uses a library that is renamed or deleted
Description: When you have a referenced model that uses a library with a sublibrary, if you rename or delete the sublibrary, you can get a failure when rebuilding the Simulink (SIM) or Real-Time Workshop (RTW) target for that referenced model. This failure can occur if you do not save the referenced model before rebuilding the targets.
Workaround: One workaround is to save and rebuild the model that failed to rebuild.Alternatively, for R2009b only, you can prevent this problem from occurring by replacing matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/rtwinfomatman.p with the attached file, rtwinfomatman_R2009b.p.This file is only compatible with R2009b. If you would like a patch for other versions of MATLAB, please contact The MathWorks Technical Support at https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do.

Summary: Error message when building AUTOSAR server operation model with atomic function subsystem or atomic reusable function subsystem
Description: If your AUTOSAR server operation model has an atomic function subsystem or atomic reusable function subsystem, you see the following error message when you try to build the model: 'mbitfield2/SubSystem' can not generate a reusable function. Consider deselecting the option 'Generate reusable code' on the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Interface page. ?
Workaround: For the atomic subsystems, set Subsystem Parameters > Real-Time Workshop system code to Inline.

Summary: Exported Simulink fixed-point data types may cause import errors in an AUTOSAR authoring tool
Description: AUTOSAR uses the min/max value of integer types to deduce the word length needed for the datatype. However, for the AUTOSAR target, the software incorrectly exports the real-world min/max value rather than the stored integer min/max value. For example, the software exports, fxp5 = Simulink.NumericType DataTypeMode: 'Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling' Signed: true WordLength: 16 Slope: 2^-5 Bias: 0 IsAlias: true HeaderFile: 'Rte_Type.h' Description: '' as: <INTEGER-TYPE> <SHORT-NAME>fxp5</SHORT-NAME> <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <COMPU-METHOD-REF DEST="COMPU-METHOD">/mTestFxpWrdSz_pkg/mTestFxpWrdSz_dt/COMPU_fxp568ed7_27c9e278c34e8e38</COMPU-METHOD-REF> </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">-1024</LOWER-LIMIT> <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">1023.96875</UPPER-LIMIT></INTEGER-TYPE> This is incorrect. The Upper-Limit and Lower-Limit should be the upper limit and lower limit of the stored integer representation: <INTEGER-TYPE> <SHORT-NAME>fxp5</SHORT-NAME> <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <COMPU-METHOD-REF DEST="COMPU-METHOD">/mTestFxpWrdSz_pkg/mTestFxpWrdSz_dt/COMPU_fxp568ed7_27c9e278c34e8e38</COMPU-METHOD-REF> </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">-32768</LOWER-LIMIT> <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">32767</UPPER-LIMIT></INTEGER-TYPE> This bug may result in the following: Import errors in an AUTOSAR authoring tool RTE generator defines an incorrect word length
Workaround: Download the attached file and use this to overwrite the existing file. Ensure that you install the correct version in the following location:/matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@exporter/private/p_export_component.p

Summary: Unnecessary name mangling of local variables that represent block outputs
Description: Prior to 2009b, local variables representing block outputs were given mangled names, even though the mangle suffix was not necessary to distinguish between two similarly named variables. For example, in rtwdemo_counter , the local variable, rtb_equal_to_count , derived from a block by the same name, does not need a mangle suffix. If a block by the same name was contained in an inner, inlined subsystem, then a mangle suffix would be needed.

Summary: When creating many figures (> 40), MATLAB can generate a Segmentation Violation error. This error requires the user to exit and restart.
Description: When creating many figures, MATLAB runs low on memory and encounters a software bug that can cause a Segmentation Violation.

Summary: Linux? platform: Printing text to Postscript? printer produces blank pages
Description: On Linux? platforms, printing text from the Editor or Command Window to a Postscript? printer may produce blank pages. This is due to a bug in how Linux software converts Postscript? data into PCL (Printer Control Language).
Workaround: There are two workarounds. One is to use a PostScript printer so that there is no translation of the Postscript data. If that is not possible, follow these steps to print the content:Select File > Print > Print to File, and then click Print to create a file.Open the newly created file in a text editor.Search for the string DeferredMediaSelection, and then delete the line that contains it. For example, delete the entire line that appears similar to: <</DeferredMediaSelection true /PageSize [612.0 792.0] /ImagingBBox null /ManualFeed false /NumCopies 1 >> setpagedevice.Save the file, and then print it using a Linux? printing program (for example, KGhostView if you're using KDE).
